about.title: About OpenHW Explorer
about.subtitle: An independent, source-checked guide to OpenHW projects, learning materials, and the wider open hardware ecosystem.

purpose.title: Why this project exists
purpose.p1: OpenHW projects, documentation, learning material, and ecosystem links live across many repositories and websites. OpenHW Explorer brings those public sources into one navigable structure.
purpose.p2: It is designed for engineers, students, and researchers who need to identify the right project, understand its scope, or move from discovery into source-grounded learning.

disclaimer.title: Independent project
disclaimer.content: OpenHW Explorer is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by the OpenHW Foundation or its member organizations. Project information is compiled from public sources and may change after review. For authoritative information, use the official project repositories and OpenHW documentation.

approach.title: How the site is curated
approach.intro: The site favors verifiable sources, practical learning paths, and clear boundaries over exhaustive coverage.

sources.title: Source-backed profiles
sources.desc: Repository profiles record primary links, review dates, and the evidence behind important technical claims.

learning.title: Learning tied to real material
learning.desc: New lessons will be published only after their explanations, sources, and presentation have been reviewed.

context.title: Curated ecosystem context
context.desc: Resources and news are selected for relevance, with source, date, and project status kept visible.

author.title: Maintained by Alex Chen
author.bio: Alex Chen maintains OpenHW Explorer as an independent project alongside his work in the OpenHW ecosystem. The site grew from a practical need to organize public project information, verify important claims against primary sources, and make OpenHW and RISC-V material easier to navigate.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.content: OpenHW Explorer has no user accounts and does not ask visitors to submit personal information. It uses Vercel Web Analytics for anonymous, aggregate page-view statistics and limited, structured client-error signals; Vercel also processes ordinary hosting request data under its own terms. This site does not set tracking cookies. Browser localStorage remembers display preferences, and feedback opens GitHub Issues.

contributePage.title: Learn and Contribute to OpenHW
contributePage.subtitle: Start with practical learning tracks, then join real OpenHW contribution workflows.

classroom.title: OpenHW Mini Classroom
classroom.subtitle: V1 focuses on guided learning tracks using open resources and real project entry points.

contribution.title: Contribution Onboarding
contribution.desc: Understand ECA, process expectations, and contribution flow before your first PR.

cores.title: Core Deep Dives
cores.desc: Start from flagship and embedded cores to understand architecture trade-offs and usage paths.

verification.title: Verification Workshop
verification.desc: Learn UVM-centric verification flow and instruction generation in the CORE-V ecosystem.

software.title: Software Enablement
software.desc: Bridge hardware and software with SDK and RTOS-oriented project paths.

classroom.futureNote: Roadmap: this section will evolve into themed PPT-based open classes (contribute flow, core usage, verification playbooks) in V2.

whyContribute.title: Why Contribute?
whyContribute.reason1: Impact real-world chip designs used in industry and academia
whyContribute.reason2: Build expertise in RISC-V architecture and verification methodologies
whyContribute.reason3: Join a welcoming community of hardware engineers and researchers
whyContribute.reason4: Get your work recognized in a major open-source hardware organization

howTo.title: How to Contribute
howTo.step1Desc: Browse the repos below and find one that matches your skills and interests.
howTo.step2Desc: Check the project's CONTRIBUTING.md and coding conventions.
howTo.step3Desc: OpenHW projects require an Eclipse Contributor Agreement (ECA).
howTo.step4Desc: Fork, create a branch, make your changes, and submit a PR.

contributePage.goodFirstIssuesDesc: Issues tagged as beginner-friendly across OpenHW repos
contributePage.noGoodFirstIssues: No good first issues found at the moment. Check back later or browse open issues directly on GitHub.

rankings.title: GitHub Repository Snapshot
rankings.disclaimer: Public GitHub signals for discovery; they are not a measure of technical quality or project maturity.

resources.ecaDesc: Required for all contributions to OpenHW projects
resources.guidelinesDesc: Official slides and documentation on the contribution process
resources.workGroupsDesc: Join a task group to collaborate with other contributors
